description: A Microsoft-developed security tool that automatically manages and randomizes the local administrator account passwords for domain-joined computers, storing them in Active Directory (AD) or Microsoft Entra ID to prevent lateral movement by attackers.
userImpact: None - As well as deploying this policy this feature needs to be enabled manually in Entra by turning on LAPS in Device Settings in the Device blade.

managementTemplateSteps.description: It is a Microsoft-developed security tool that automatically manages and randomizes the local administrator account passwords for domain-joined computers, storing them in Active Directory (AD) or Microsoft Entra ID to prevent lateral movement by attackers.

settings.name: description
settings.displayName: Enabling LAPS for all Entra Joined devices removing admin privileges for all users

referenceStrings.name: device_vendor_msft_laps_policies_backupdirectory_1
referenceStrings.displayName: Backup the password to Microsoft Entra ID only

referenceStrings.name: device_vendor_msft_laps_policies_passwordcomplexity_4
referenceStrings.displayName: Large letters + small letters + numbers + special characters

referenceStrings.name: device_vendor_msft_laps_policies_postauthenticationactions_3
referenceStrings.displayName: Reset the password and logoff the managed account: upon expiry of the grace period, the managed account password will be reset and any interactive logon sessions using the managed account will terminated.

referenceStrings.name: device_vendor_msft_laps_policies_automaticaccountmanagementenabled_false
referenceStrings.displayName: The target account will not be automatically managed
